figures.caption: Table 2: SFT encourages supertoken adoption, reducing reasoning trace length while preserving accuracy across three model families and five math benchmarks. All accuracy deltas fall within the 95% confidence interval of zero; ˚ marks token reductions whose 95% CI excludes zero (p ă 0.05). See Appendix J for methodology. Table 9.
Table 9: Wall-clock latency (mean seconds per sample) for baseline vs. SFT models, served via SGLang or VLLM on H200 GPUs. Latency reductions exceed or track token count reductions from Table 2.

Table 3: Supertoken structural taxonomy. Each merge in the SuperBPE vocabulary is classified into one of nine structural categories based on its decoded text. Representative supertokens are shown for each category.

Table 10: Mean conditional entropy (bits) by token role for QwQ-32B reasoning traces (2K samples, 22.9M tokens), scored by the generating model (QwQ-32B, self) vs. a different model (Qwen3-30B-A3B, cross). The structural/organic entropy gap (bottom row) persists and widens under cross-model scoring, confirming the pattern is not an artifact of a single model’s distribution.

Figure 1: Overview of the pipeline: (1) derive reasoning-specific supertokens via BPE merges on reasoning traces, then (2) adapt the model to utilize these supertokens via supervised fine-tuning to produce shorter reasoning trajectories while preserving accuracy.

Table 8: LoRA rank ablation on Qwen3-30B-A3B. All runs use α“32, targets tq,k,v,ou proj, with embed/lm head as modules to save. Supertoken usage is consistent across ranks („10–13%); accuracy is robust to rank choice.
Figure 6: Token compression rate based on merge selection rules and number of merges included.

Table 12: 95% confidence intervals for accuracy and completion-token deltas. Accuracy: twoproportion CI treating each condition as a single evaluation on N questions (conservative). All 15 accuracy CIs include zero. Completion tokens: paired-difference CI with estimated σpaired « 0.24 ˆ T̄. ✓ marks token reductions whose CI excludes zero. Reductions are significant on larger benchmarks; AIME (N“30) lacks power except for the large DS-R1-70B compressions.
Table 5: Representative super-tokens from unfiltered and filtered BPE. The filter removes syntactically promiscuous merges and math artifacts, and by admitting apostrophes surfaces

Figure 3: Supertoken signposts reveal reasoning structure. Each block represents a structural reasoning move detected via supertokens. The correct trace (top) shows diverse, balanced reasoning with verification; the incorrect trace (bottom) is dominated by repeated backtracking without verification.

Table 1: Comparison with CoT compression methods. Content-level and latent methods achieve higher compression but alter or remove reasoning steps. Our vocabulary-level approach preserves the complete readable trace and is orthogonal to content-level methods (the two can be composed). ˚Relative to respective baselines on MATH-500.

Figure 7: Token-level conditional entropy for two windows from QwQ-32B reasoning traces (blue = low, red = high). Black brackets mark supertoken merge spans (ě3 base tokens). Within bracketed spans, continuation tokens are predominantly low-entropy, confirming that structural phrases are near-deterministic once begun (continuation mean: 1.03 bits vs. non-merge mean: 1.27 bits).
Table 7: Comparison of SFT training strategies on QWQ-32B. Partial unfreezing achieves the best compression while maintaining accuracy.

Figure 9: Theoretical compression ceiling. (a) Mean entropy by token role (continuation: 1.03, merge heads: 1.11, non-merged: 1.27 bits), all well below the vocabulary maximum of 17.2 bits. (b) Compression ceiling decomposition (∆ “ ρ ˆ p1 ´ H̄{ log2 |V |q). Compressibility is „94% across all roles; the combined ceiling ∆ “ 14.3%.

Figure 2: Continuation-token entropy by supertoken merge length. (a) Mean conditional entropy per bin (dashed line: non-merge average, 1.27 bits). The gap widens from 33% at length 2 to 79% at length 16+; length-3 merges are an outlier (´5%). (b) Relative entropy reduction (1 ´ H̄cont{H̄non-merge); longer merges capture progressively more deterministic continuations.

Table 6: Taxonomy classification rules applied in priority order. N = number of the 250 supertokens assigned to each category. All 250 merges are classified; no merges fall into an “other” residual category.
